## 11. MOBILE-FIRST UX FOR LATAM

### The Mobile Reality
- 81% of e-commerce sales in Paraguay come from mobile devices
- 91% of internet users shop via mobile in LATAM
- 75%+ of online purchases in LATAM occur via smartphones
- Mobile commerce projected to reach $100B in LATAM by 2025
- Paraguay has 7.72M mobile connections (111.8% penetration)
- Median mobile speed: 17.65 Mbps (optimize for moderate speeds)

### Critical Mobile UX Requirements
1. Thumb-friendly navigation: CTAs in bottom-center (reachable zone)
2. Fast loading: <3 seconds max (53% leave if >3s, each second costs 20% conversions)
3. Responsive design: Must work on small screens with touch
4. Simplified checkout: Guest checkout, autofill, minimal fields
5. Sticky CTAs: "Add to Cart" always visible while scrolling
6. Vertical scrolling design: Image -> Price/CTA -> Description hierarchy
7. Image optimization: WebP/AVIF format, lazy loading, compressed
8. Large touch targets: Minimum 48x48px with 8px spacing
9. Search prominent: Above fold with autocomplete/predictive
10. Gesture support: Swipe for galleries, pinch-to-zoom
11. Local payment methods: QR codes (Bancard), installment options, digital wallets
12. WhatsApp integration: Click-to-chat, support, order updates

### Trust Signals for LATAM Mobile Users
- 80% of LATAM users don't feel confident providing personal info online
- Display security badges, reviews, testimonials
- Multi-factor authentication and biometrics
- Clear return/refund policies
- WhatsApp customer support visible
- Live chat option

### Performance Optimization
- Core Web Vitals compliance (LCP <2.5s, FID <100ms, CLS <0.1)
- Image compression
- CSS/JS minification
- Browser caching
- CDN usage
- Test on real 4G networks, not just desktop emulation

## 12. EMAIL/SMS MARKETING REGULATIONS IN PARAGUAY

### Current Legal Framework
- Law No. 6534/2020 -- Personal Credit Data Protection Law (currently active)
- Law No. 4868/2013 -- Electronic Commerce Law (+ Regulatory Decree 1165/2014)
- Law No. 7593/25 -- NEW General Personal Data Protection Law (passed Nov 2025, effective Nov 2027)
- Conatel -- National Telecommunications Commission (oversees SMS/telecom)
- SEDECO -- Secretariat for Consumer Defense
- BCP -- Central Bank of Paraguay (enforces credit data provisions)

### SMS Marketing Requirements
1. Explicit consent required -- Written or electronic, documented for minimum 2 years
2. Consent language must be provided in BOTH Spanish and Guarani
3. Opt-out keywords must be supported:
   - Spanish: STOP, PARAR, BASTA
   - Guarani: ANICHEMA
4. Opt-out confirmation must be sent within 24 hours
5. Maintain suppression lists -- Remove opted-out numbers within 24 hours
6. Time zone sensitivity -- No messages during late night hours
7. No official Do Not Call registry -- but maintain own lists

### Prohibited SMS Content
- Gambling and betting services
- Adult content or explicit material
- Unauthorized financial services
- Political campaign messages without authorization
- Religious content without explicit consent

### Regulated Industries
- Financial services: require CONATEL approval
- Healthcare: must comply with privacy regulations
- Educational institutions: need proper accreditation

### Email Marketing Requirements
- Electronic Commerce Law requires:
  - Clear information about data collection purposes
  - Opt-out mechanism (simple and free)
  - Express consent for personal data collection
- No specific anti-spam law, but general data protection applies
- Best practice: double opt-in, unsubscribe link in every email, maintain consent records

### New Law No. 7593/25 (Effective Nov 2027)
- Aligns Paraguay with EU GDPR and LATAM peers (Brazil LGPD, etc.)
- Creates National Personal Data Protection Agency
- Requires DPO appointment
- Mandatory breach notification within 72 hours
- Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing
- International transfer restrictions
- Individual rights: access, rectification, deletion, portability, objection
- Applies to any company offering goods/services in Paraguay
- Fines and sanctions possible
- ACTION: Begin compliance preparation now; law takes effect in 18 months

### Recommended Compliance Checklist for El Viajero
- Obtain explicit consent before any marketing communication
- Maintain consent records (method, timestamp, source) for 2+ years
- Implement STOP/PARAR/BASTA/ANICHEMA opt-out in SMS
- Unsubscribe link in ALL email marketing
- Privacy policy compliant with Law 6534/2020
- Cookie consent mechanism
- Begin GDPR-style compliance for Law 7593/25 (DPO, DPIA, breach notification)
- Bilingual opt-in/opt-out in Spanish and Guarani
- Document data processing activities
- Review SEDECO and CONATEL regulations periodically

### New Law No. 7593/25 (Effective Nov 2027)
- Aligns Paraguay with EU GDPR and LATAM peers (Brazil LGPD, etc.)
- Creates National Personal Data Protection Agency
- Requires DPO appointment
- Mandatory breach notification within 72 hours
- Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing
- International transfer restrictions
- Individual rights: access, rectification, deletion, portability, objection
- Applies to any company offering goods/services in Paraguay
- Fines and sanctions possible
- ACTION: Begin compliance preparation now; law takes effect in 18 months

### Recommended Compliance Checklist for El Viajero
- Obtain explicit consent before any marketing communication
- Maintain consent records (method, timestamp, source) for 2+ years
- Implement STOP/PARAR/BASTA/ANICHEMA opt-out in SMS
- Unsubscribe link in ALL email marketing
- Privacy policy compliant with Law 6534/2020
- Cookie consent mechanism
- Begin GDPR-style compliance for Law 7593/25 (DPO, DPIA, breach notification)
- Bilingual opt-in/opt-out in Spanish and Guarani
- Document data processing activities
- Review SEDECO and CONATEL regulations periodically

---

## 13. EL VIAJERO: SPECIFIC GAP ANALYSIS

### Currently Implemented (Good Foundation)
- Basic SEO metadata
- JSON-LD schema
- sitemap.xml
- RSS feed
- Blog (6 posts)
- Footer newsletter signup
- WhatsApp float button

### Critical Gaps to Address (Priority Order)

Immediate (Week 1-2):
1. Google Search Console setup + Bing Webmaster Tools
2. Google Analytics 4 with enhanced e-commerce tracking
3. Google Business Profile (GMB) creation/optimization
4. Register on Paraguayan directories (Paginas Amarillas, Infonegocios)
5. Facebook Shops / Instagram Shopping setup

Short-term (Month 1-2):
6. Product structured data (schema.org/Product)
7. BreadcrumbList schema
8. FAQ schema for blog posts
9. Review schema for products
10. Spanish language SEO content (blog posts targeting local keywords)
11. Meta Ads campaign with WhatsApp integration
12. MercadoLibre seller account + Product Ads

Medium-term (Month 2-3):
13. WhatsApp Business API integration (via Woztell or Unify360)
14. Abandoned cart automation via WhatsApp
15. Influencer partnerships (micro-influencers, 5K-50K followers)
16. TikTok content strategy (short-form video)
17. Guarani language content testing
18. Voice search optimization (FAQs, conversational content)

Long-term (Month 3-6):
19. Mobile-first UX redesign (if needed)
20. Core Web Vitals optimization
21. Law 7593/25 compliance preparation
22. Local link-building campaign
23. Email marketing automation with segmentation
24. SMS marketing (with proper consent/compliance)
25. Installment payment options (cuotas via Bancard/Mercado Pago)

---

## 14. KEY RECOMMENDATIONS SUMMARY

1. Mobile-first is non-negotiable -- 81% of sales are mobile
2. WhatsApp is the primary engagement channel -- integrate API for automation
3. MercadoLibre is the dominant marketplace -- 65% share, must be on it
4. Local SEO is critical -- GMB + local directories + Paraguayan Spanish keywords
5. Micro-influencers outperform macro-influencers in Paraguay for conversion
6. TikTok is the fastest-growing platform -- 75.3% of adults
7. Bilingual content (Spanish/Guarani) builds trust and authenticity
8. Voice search optimization is an untapped opportunity
9. Installment payments (cuotas) are essential for conversion
10. Start GDPR-style compliance NOW before Law 7593/25 takes effect
11. Combine SEO + SEM for immediate and sustainable results
12. UGC and short-form video are the highest-performing content formats

Research compiled via web search and extraction from: DataReportal, PCMI, ABC Color Paraguay, RankTracker, Nexo Digital, Statista, NapoleonCat, Capace, Mercado Libre, Sent.dm, DLA Piper, various LATAM marketing agencies.
"}}

messages.content:
{"result": "# El Viajero - Business & Growth Opportunities Research Report
## Outdoor/Camping E-Commerce Store - Mariano Roque Alonso, Paraguay
### Founded 2018 | Sells: Camping, Fishing, Auto, Moto, Outdoor

---

## 1. MARKET CONTEXT

Paraguay's e-commerce market reached **USD 700 million in 2024** (growing 32% YoY) and is projected to hit **USD 1.5 billion by 2027** (28% CAGR). Key insight: **77% of e-commerce sales go to foreign stores** -- domestic players with strong local pickup have a built-in advantage.

Tourism is booming: **2.25 million international tourists in 2025** (+84% YoY), making Paraguay the world's fastest-growing tourist destination. Domestic tourism surged **103%**. The LATAM "open to adventure" travel market is valued at **USD 39 billion**. Camping goods in LATAM are growing at **2.2% CAGR volume / 2.5% CAGR value** through 2035.

---

## 2. B2B / CORPORATE SALES

**Opportunity:** Companies in Paraguay organize corporate retreats, team-building, and MICE events. The MICE segment alone generated **USD 500 million** in economic impact in 2025, with 236 events.

**Strategy:**
- Create "Company Retreat in a Box" packages (bulk camping kits for 10-50 people)
- Volume discounts starting at 10+ units on tents, sleeping bags, cooking gear, coolers
- Target HR departments, event planners, schools, scouts, churches, sports clubs
- LinkedIn outreach + presence at MICE events/FITPAR

**Revenue potential:** A single corporate order of 20 full kits at ~$100/kit = $2,000. Target 5-10 corporate clients/month.

---

## 3. BULK / CUSTOM ORDERS

**Strategy:**
- Offer custom embroidery/printing on backpacks, coolers, chairs, hats
- Branded survival kits for corporate gifting
- Act as procurement agent for NGOs, government, schools
- Partner with local screen printers or invest in DTG/embroidery machine

**Margin:** 25-40% on bulk vs 50-100% retail

---

## 4. LOCAL PICKUP EXPERIENCE IMPROVEMENTS

This is a high-impact, low-effort opportunity. Physical store + e-commerce = competitive moat against pure online players.

**Recommendations:**
1. Real-time inventory sync between POS and website
2. Dedicated pickup counter/window (separate from walk-in traffic)
3. SMS/WhatsApp auto-notifications when orders are ready (Paraguay has extremely high WhatsApp penetration)
4. 2-3 reserved parking spots near store entrance with clear signage
5. Same-day pickup cutoff at 4 PM with 2-hour prep window
6. One employee dedicated per shift to manage pickup orders
7. Signage with phone number for customers to call/text on arrival

---

## 5. WARRANTY & REPAIR SERVICES

**This is a blue ocean opportunity** -- no dedicated outdoor gear repair service exists in the Mariano Roque Alonso / Asuncion area.

**Services:**
- Warranty facilitation (ship to manufacturers on behalf of customers, $5-10 handling fee)
- In-house repairs: zipper replacement, tent patches, strap repairs, sleeping bag restoration
- Gear restoration: DWR reapplication, down bag cleaning
- Custom modifications: adding pockets, shortening straps

**Benchmark:** Gear re-Store (US/Canada) processes 70,000-80,000 units/year for 30+ brands including REI, Patagonia, Arc'teryx. Rainy Pass Repair has operated since 1986.

**For El Viajero:** Start with basic sewing + warranty handling. Target 30-50 repairs/month at $15-20 average = $450-$1,000/month incremental revenue. Strong environmental/community messaging: "Keep your gear alive."

---

## 6. RENTAL PROGRAM

**Proven model in Latin America.** Chilean Patagonia rental businesses charge $7-17/day for tents, $7-8/day for sleeping bags, $20-56/day for full kits.

**Target items for rental:**
- Family tents (6-8 person)
- Premium coolers (rotomolded like Yeti)
- Sleeping bags (extreme weather)
- Backpacking stoves
- Fishing rods and tackle
- Camping chairs and tables
- Portable power stations/generators

**Suggested pricing:**
- Family tent: $15/day or $60/week
- 2-person tent: $8/day or $30/week
- Sleeping bag: $5/day or $20/week
- Full family kit (tent+4 bags+mats+stove+table+chairs): $40/day or $150/week

**ROI:** A $200 tent rented 20 times/year = $400/year revenue (200% of cost in year 1). With 10 rental transactions/week at $25 average = ~$13,000/year incremental. Requires 10-15 core rental items.

---

## 7. GUIDED TRIPS & EXPERIENCES

**Context:** Paraguay is the world's fastest-growing tourist destination. Domestic tourism is up 103%. Outdoor eco-tourism and adventure are major growth segments.

**Models:**
- Partner with local guides (60/40 or 70/30 revenue share)
- Day trips: hiking, fishing, camping excursions
- Weekend expeditions: fully outfitted guided camping
- "Intro to Camping" weekends for beginners
- Fishing charters with local experts

**Pricing:** Day trips $30-50/person, weekend $80-150/person, multi-day $200-500/person.

**Keep it asset-light:** Partner with existing guides and transport providers.

---

## 8. CROSS-BORDER SELLING (ARGENTINA/BRAZIL)

### This is arguably the single biggest growth opportunity.

**Why Paraguay is uniquely positioned:**
- Lowest tax burden in South America (15% of GDP)
- Brazilians can cross freely with USD $500 tax-free import limit (land) or $1,000 (air)
- Argentina eliminated taxes on overseas purchases up to $400 and raised import limit to $3,000
- Argentine outdoor gear is 30-50% more expensive locally

### Strategy for Brazil
1. Open a small pickup/delivery point in **Ciudad del Este** near the Friendship Bridge
2. Accept **Brazilian Real (BRL) and PIX** (140M+ active users)
3. Create **Portuguese-language** content and product pages
4. Partner with **3XS International** (UPS reseller in Paraguay-Brazil corridor)
5. Use **ParaguayBox / NextParcel** for last-mile delivery into Brazil

### Strategy for Argentina
1. Offer cross-border shipping with pre-calculated duties
2. List on **Mercado Libre Argentina** (24.8M monthly visits, $9.92B GMV)
3. Use **nocnoc** platform for simplified cross-border sales

**Revenue potential:** Cross-border could add 30-50% incremental sales within 12-18 months.

---

## 9. AFFILIATE PROGRAM

**Market benchmarks:**
- Niche outdoor brands: 10-15% commission
- Mid-tier: 8-12%
- Mass-market: 3-5%

**Strategy:** Launch at 10% commission. Target Paraguayan/LATAM outdoor micro-influencers (500-10K followers). Start with 5-10 partners doing gear loan + discount codes. Use referral codes + WhatsApp initially.

---

## 10. WHOLESALE TO OTHER STORES

**Model:** Smaller retail stores lack import capabilities. El Viajero can become a regional distributor.

**Target:** Ferreterias, multi-service shops, gas stations along major highways.

**Strategy:**
- B2B catalog (40-50% below retail)
- Minimum order $200-500
- Focus on fast-moving items: gas canisters, fishing tackle, basic chairs, coolers, hammocks
- Offer drop-shipping to other e-commerces

---

## 11. CONTENT MARKETING

**Pillars:** Destination guides, gear reviews, how-to content, safety/skills, community stories, seasonal content.

**Top content ideas for Paraguay:**
- "Los 10 Mejores Lugares para Acampar en Paraguay"
- "Que Llevar en tu Mochila para un Finde de Camping"
- "Guia de Pesca en Paraguay: Especies y Lugares"
- "Comparativa: Carpa Coleman vs Nautika - Cual Elegir?"
- "Como Armar tu Carpa en 5 Minutos"

---

## 12. SOCIAL MEDIA STRATEGY

| Platform | Primary Use | Frequency |
|----------|-------------|-----------|
| Instagram | Visual storytelling, Reels, UGC | 3-5x/week |
| Facebook | Community group, events, local reach | Daily |
| YouTube | Gear reviews, destination guides | 1-2x/month |
| TikTok | Quick tips, trends | 2-3x/week |
| WhatsApp | Direct sales, customer service | Daily |

**Local hashtags:** #CampingParaguay #OutdoorPy #AventuraParaguay #ViajeroPy #CampingLATAM

---

## 13. COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE

| Competitor | Founded | Location | Notes |
|-----------|---------|----------|-------|
| Camping 44 | 1990 | Asuncion | Largest, firearms/hunting |
| El Mohicano | - | Online | Camping + fishing |
| Chaco Outdoors | 2008 | Loma Plata + MRA | Hunting/fishing focus |
| Acacia Camping | - | Online | Survival gear |
| Gran 7 | - | Online | General + camping |
| Qualiplaza | - | Online | Camping + BBQ |
| CellShop | - | CDE | Camping + electronics |

**El Viajero's advantages:** Modern (2018), multi-category, physical+e-commerce hybrid, strategic MRA location.

---

## 14. ACTION PRIORITY MATRIX

| Opportunity | Effort | Impact | Timeline | Priority |
|-------------|--------|--------|----------|----------|
| Local Pickup | Low | Medium | 1-2 weeks | HIGH |
| WhatsApp Commerce | Low | High | 1 week | HIGH |
| Rental Program | Medium | Medium | 1-2 months | HIGH |
| Repair/Warranty | Medium | Medium | 1-2 months | HIGH |
| Content Marketing | Medium | High | Ongoing | HIGH |
| B2B Corporate | Low | Medium | 2-4 weeks | MEDIUM |
| Affiliate Program | Low | Low-Med | 1 month | MEDIUM |
| Social Media | Medium | High | Ongoing | MEDIUM |
| Cross-Border Brazil | High | Very High | 3-6 months | MEDIUM |
| Cross-Border Argentina | High | High | 3-6 months | LOW |
| Bulk/Custom Orders | Low | Low | 2-4 weeks | LOW |
| Guided Trips | High | Medium | 3-6 months | LOW |
| Wholesale to Stores | Medium | Medium | 3-6 months | LOW |

---

## 15. 90-DAY ACTION PLAN

**Month 1 - Quick Wins:**
- WhatsApp Business API for automated order confirmations
- Designated pickup area with signage
- Instagram content calendar (3-5 posts/week)
- Corporate sales one-pager PDF
- 2 blog posts

**Month 2 - Core Services:**
- Rental program with 10 initial items
- Repair service (partner with seamstress)
- Affiliate program with 5 affiliates
- YouTube channel with 3 videos
- Real-time inventory sync

**Month 3 - Growth & Cross-Border:**
- Evaluate CDE pickup point or partnership
- Portuguese-language content for Brazil
- Contact 3XS International for logistics
- Accept BRL + PIX payments
- Outreach to 10 corporate clients
- Launch "Comunidad Outdoor Paraguay" Facebook Group

---

*Report generated May 8, 2026. Sources: PCMI, UN Tourism, Senatur Paraguay, IndexBox, ATTA, 6W Research, industry publications.*

function.arguments:
{"path": "/root/elviajero/docs/09-market-intelligence/01-tech-architecture-audit.md", "content": "# El Viajero — Tech Architecture & Code Audit

Date: May 8, 2026
Audit depth: Full source-level analysis

---

## 1. Build Baseline

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Node modules total | 554MB |
| Standalone build | 69MB |
| Pages (static + dynamic) | 88 routes |
| Components | 103 files |
| Lib modules | 31 files |
| API routes | 25+ |
| Content file | 71KB / 1990 lines |
| Docker image | 365MB |
| RAM limit | 512MB |
| Replicas | 2 |

---

## 2. @ai-whisperers Packages — Value vs Bloat

### Actually imported in source code:
| Package | Used in | What for |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `@ai-whisperers/auth` | 20+ files | AuthProvider, useAuth, createClient, createAdminClient |
| `@ai-whisperers/commerce` | 14 files | useCart, CartProvider, CurrencySwitcher, types |

### NOT imported anywhere in source code (dead weight):
| Package | Size | Status |
|---------|------|--------|
| `@ai-whisperers/admin` | 116K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/api-helpers` | 84K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/catalog` | source | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/checkout` | source | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/i18n` | 220K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/payments` | 36K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/product` | source | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/seo` | 52K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/theme` | 84K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/ui-extras` | 324K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` | 52K | ZERO imports |
| `@ai-whisperers/product` (devDep) | source | Duplicate |
| `@ai-whisperers/checkout` (devDep) | source | Duplicate |
| `@ai-whisperers/catalog` (devDep) | source | Duplicate |
| `@ai-whisperers/ui-extras` (devDep) | source | Duplicate |
| `@ai-whisperers/payments` (devDep) | source | Duplicate |
| `@ai-whisperers/api-helpers` (devDep) | source | Duplicate |

**11 of 17 packages are dead weight.** 6 are duplicated in both deps and devDeps.

### Component-level duplication
Every export from unused packages has an identical local implementation:
- 19 components match `@ai-whisperers/product`
- 9 components match `@ai-whisperers/commerce` (but commerce IS used)
- 7 components match `@ai-whisperers/catalog`
- 16 components match `@ai-whisperers/ui-extras`

**Recommendation:** Remove all 17 @ai-whisperers package references. The 2 actually-used packages are thin wrappers over Supabase + React context that could be inlined. This removes the GitHub npm registry dependency, NODE_AUTH_TOKEN requirement, and simplifies deployment.

---

## 3. Content Duplication

`content/es.json` has the same product data duplicated in two sections:
- `home.productCatalog.products` — 34 products
- `home.featuredProducts.products` — same products, subset
- `productos.list` — same 34 products again

~12KB of pure duplication. Products are also stored in Supabase (ej_products table with 34 rows). The content file products appear to be a static fallback that's never used since the API routes query Supabase directly.

**Recommendation:** Remove static product data from content/es.json. Keep only page copy, navigation, and UI text. Products should be 100% Supabase-driven.

---

## 4. Security Issues

| Severity | Issue | Location |
|----------|-------|----------|
| CRITICAL | Hardcoded Supabase keys in version control | docker-compose.yml lines 13-14 |
| HIGH | GitHub PAT in .npmrc (not gitignored) | .npmrc |
| MEDIUM | No Content-Security-Policy header | next.config.ts |
| LOW | NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars baked into Docker build | Dockerfile ARG/ENV |
| LOW | TypeScript shipped in production image | 8.7MB typescript.js in node_modules |

---

## 5. Build & Infrastructure

### Dockerfile issues
- TypeScript compiler shipped to production (8.7MB)
- NODE_AUTH_TOKEN build-time dependency blocks CI without VPN
- NEXT_PUBLIC_ env vars are build-time ARGs instead of runtime ENV

### Docker compose issues
- 2 replicas at 512MB each is tight for Node.js 20 standalone
- Keys in compose file should be docker secrets or .env file
- Traefik labels work but port mapping 3000 is unused (swarm routes via network)

### Deployment
- deploy.sh builds + deploys but doesn't tag with git hash for rollback
- No CI/CD pipeline beyond the single script
- No canary or blue-green deploy

---

## 6. Code Quality

### Mixed import patterns
Some files import from `@ai-whisperers/auth/supabase/*` while others use local `lib/supabase/*` equivalents. Both exist with identical implementations.

### Large client components
The homepage (`app/page.tsx`) is 373 lines of client-side code with inline state management. Should be split into smaller components.

### Missing error boundaries
Most API routes have try/catch but no structured error responses. The admin panel shows skeleton loaders but no error recovery.

### Tests
Jest configured with 2 test files found in `__tests__/` — minimal coverage for a 88-page application.

---

## 7. Performance

### Good
- Most page chunks are 20-28KB (client bundle)
- Static pages where possible (most pages are prerendered)
- Image optimization (Next.js Image component)
- Standalone output mode

### Needs work
- 188KB framework chunk is large (could code-split)
- No bundle analysis ran
- No Core Web Vitals monitoring
- No image CDN beyond Next.js built-in
- No service worker / PWA
- No lazy loading for below-fold content
"}

{"content": "# El Viajero — Paraguayan E-Commerce Market & Competitor Research

Date: May 8, 2026

---

## 1. Paraguay Digital Landscape

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Population | 6.90M (median age 26.3) |
| Internet users | 5.62M (81.3%) |
| Social media users | 4.22M (61.1%) |
| Mobile connections | 7.72M (111.8%) |
| E-commerce market 2024 | $700M USD |
| YoY growth | 32% |
| Projected 2027 | $1.5B (28% CAGR) |
| Mobile sales share | 81% |
| Cross-border share | 77% |
| Monthly online shoppers | 4/10 adults |

### Shopping channels used
- Website: 100%
- Social media: 66%
- WhatsApp: 46%

### Payment methods
1. Credit card: 61%
2. Debit card: 15%
3. Bank transfer: 9%
4. Digital wallets: 5%
5. Cash: 3%

---

## 2. Direct Competitors

### Decathlon Paraguay
- Massive catalog, cheapest prices
- Biggest threat on basics (tents, bags, mats)
- Limited niche/specialty gear

### Camping 44
- Founded 1990, Asuncion
- Broadest outdoor range incl. firearms/hunting
- Physical store + basic website
- 34-year reputation

### Chaco Outdoors
- Founded 2008, Loma Plata + Mariano Roque Alonso
- Direct neighbor (same city as El Viajero)
- Hunting/fishing focus, strong in tactical gear

### El Mohicano
- Online-only, Shopify store
- Camping + fishing focus
- Prices match El Viajero's range (Gs. 35k-2M)

### Pesca Pro Paraguay
- Fishing specialist
- Small camping section
- WooCommerce platform

### PC Survival Shop
- 539 camping items
- Tactical/hunting focus
- WooCommerce

### CellShop (CDE)
- Mega-department store, Ciudad del Este
- Premium brands, Magento platform
- 12-installment payment plans
- Camping + electronics

### Qualiplaza
- General e-commerce
- Coleman/Weber premium camping
- Broader catalog dilutes outdoor focus

### Gran 7
- Discount generalist
- WhatsApp-first ordering model
- Cash-on-delivery payment

**El Viajero's pricing (Gs. 35k-2M):** Middle of the market. Above Decathlon budget basics, below CellShop premium. Strong position for "good quality at fair price."

---

## 3. Camping/Outdoor Market Size (Paraguay)

| Estimate | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Conservative | $15-25M USD |
| Inclusive (fishing/hunting) | $30-50M USD |
| Annual growth | 10-15% |
| Key drivers | Eco-tourism boom, domestic tourism +103% YoY |

---

## 4. Payment Gateways for LATAM E-Commerce

### Pagopar (Recommended as primary)
- Aggregates: credit/debit cards, cash (6,500+ points), e-wallets, QR, PIX
- Has WooCommerce/Shopify plugins
- Already configured in docker-compose.yml (keys not set)
- Best coverage for Paraguay

### Bancard
- Paraguay's national card processor
- ~3% fee
- Required for installment plans (cuotas)
- Already configured (keys not set)

### Stripe
- NOT reliably available for PY-local merchants
- Only use for international/cross-border

### Mercado Pago
- 70% of LATAM digital payments
- Integrated with MercadoLibre
- Strong in Argentina/Brazil
- Installment plans standard

### PIX (Brazil)
- 140M+ active users
- Instant settlement
- Essential for Brazilian cross-border sales

---

## 5. WhatsApp Commerce Patterns

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| LATAM consumers prefer messaging | 85% |
| Conversion improvement | 4.2x vs traditional |
| Higher average tickets | 35% |
| Dominant model | Cash-on-delivery + payment links via chat |

### WhatsApp automation tools available in Paraguay
1. **Unify360** — CRM with WhatsApp API, AI chatbots, lead assignment
2. **Woztell** (LOGIN S.A.) — ChatGPT + WhatsApp, e-commerce ordering
3. **OpenWhats** — Mass messaging, chatbot, anti-block
4. **ChatPro/Kommo** — Omnichannel CRM (WhatsApp+FB+IG)
5. **Wopy** — Paraguayan-built, WhatsApp ordering, QR payments, local payment integrations
6. **AutoWabot** — Mass WhatsApp messaging
7. **MFormula** — Campaigns, chatbots, CRM

---

## 6. Shipping & Logistics Landscape

- Major carriers in Paraguay: Aerobox, ParaguayBox, NextParcel
- Cross-border specialists: 3XS International (UPS authorized, 24h customs clearance Paraguay-Brazil)
- Local delivery: Rappi, Pickio, PedidosYa (mostly food, expanding)
- Most e-commerce stores use in-house delivery or moto-mensajeria
- Free shipping threshold is a standard conversion tactic
- Same-day delivery in Asuncion metro area is a differentiator
", "path": "/root/elviajero/docs/09-market-intelligence/02-market-competitor-analysis.md"}

{"content": "# El Viajero — E-Commerce Features Gap Analysis

Date: May 8, 2026
Comparison: Current state vs best-in-class LATAM e-commerce benchmarks

---

## Feature Status Matrix

| Feature | Status | Priority | Impact | Notes |
|---------|--------|----------|--------|-------|
| Shopping cart | ✅ Done | — | — | Basic cart with sidebar |
| Product catalog | ✅ Done | — | — | 6 categories, 34 products |
| Search & filters | ✅ Done | — | — | Autocomplete, category filters |
| User accounts | ✅ Done | — | — | Supabase auth, profile mgmt |
| Blog | ✅ Done | — | — | 6 posts, categories |
| Admin panel | ✅ Done | — | — | 15 pages |
| WhatsApp integration | ✅ Done | — | — | Float button, manual |
| Newsletter | ✅ Done | — | — | Footer signup |
| Promo codes | ✅ Done | — | — | Via Supabase |
| Product reviews | ✅ Done | — | — | With ratings |
| Recently viewed | ✅ Done | — | — | localStorage |
| Compare products | ✅ Done | — | — | Basic implementation |
| Exit intent popup | ✅ Done | — | — | With offer |
| Cookie consent | ✅ Done | — | — | EU-style |
| Dark mode | ✅ Done | — | — | Toggle |
| Currency toggle | ✅ Done | — | — | PYG/USD |
| Language toggle | ✅ Done | — | — | ES/EN/GN |
| Order tracking | ✅ Basic | P1 | High | Basic order status |
| — | — | — | — | — |
| **Live payments** | ❌ Missing | **P0** | **Blocking** | Pagopar/Bancard keys not set |
| **Abandoned cart recovery** | ❌ Missing | **P0** | **High** | Cart recovery API exists but inactive |
| **Shipping calculator** | ❌ Missing | **P0** | **High** | 63% abandon due to surprise shipping |
| Google Analytics 4 | ❌ Missing | P1 | High | No conversion tracking |
| Google Search Console | ❌ Missing | P1 | High | No search performance data |
| Product schema markup | ❌ Missing | P1 | High | Missing from product pages |
| Breadcrumb schema | ❌ Missing | P1 | Medium | Affects SERP display |
| FAQ schema | ❌ Missing | P1 | Medium | For blog/FAQ pages |
| Review schema | ❌ Missing | P1 | Medium | Stars in search results |
| Google Business Profile | ❌ Missing | P1 | High | Not claimed/optimized |
| MercadoLibre listing | ❌ Missing | P1 | High | 65% market share |
| Facebook Shops | ❌ Missing | P1 | Medium | Integrated checkout |
| WhatsApp Business API | ❌ Missing | P1 | High | Manual only, no automation |
| Push notifications | ❌ Missing | P2 | Medium | Browser push |
| Customer segmentation | ❌ Missing | P2 | Medium | RFM-based marketing |
| Email automation | ❌ Missing | P2 | Medium | Behavioral flows |
| Order tracking UX | ❌ Missing | P2 | Medium | Branded tracking page |
| PWA / offline mode | ❌ Missing | P2 | Medium | Service worker |
| Loyalty program | ❌ Missing | P2 | Low-Med | Points system |
| B2B / bulk pricing | ❌ Missing | P2 | Medium | Volume discounts |
| Rental booking | ❌ Missing | P3 | Medium | Gear rental |
| Multi-warehouse | ❌ Missing | P3 | Low | Single location |
| POS integration | ❌ Missing | P3 | Low | Real-time inventory sync |

---

## P0 — Blocking Revenue

### 1. Live Payments
- Pagopar and Bancard configured in docker-compose.yml but keys not set
- No Stripe keys either
- Site cannot process real payments
- **Fix:** Set Pagopar public/private keys + Bancard keys from client

### 2. Abandoned Cart Recovery
- 70% cart abandonment industry average
- Best-in-class recovers 18-22%
- For a store with the current traffic: potentially $X,XXX/month in recovered revenue
- **Quick win:** Use existing WhatsApp integration to send cart reminder messages
- Cart recovery API endpoint already exists at `/api/cart-recovery`

### 3. Shipping Calculator
- 63% of cart abandonment due to unexpected shipping costs
- No live calculation during checkout
- **Fix:** API endpoint exists at `/api/shipping` but needs carrier integration

---

## P1 — High Impact

### Analytics & Measurement
- GA4 enhanced e-commerce tracking would reveal: conversion funnel leaks, top products, customer lifetime value
- GSC needed for search performance data
- Meta Pixel (now Conversion API) for ad attribution

### Structured Data (SEO)
- Product schema (review stars, price, availability in search results)
- BreadcrumbList (breadcrumb in SERP)
- FAQ schema (expandable in search)
- Review schema (star ratings in SERP)

### Marketplaces & Social Commerce
- MercadoLibre = 65% of Paraguayan e-commerce. Not being on it means leaving money on the table
- Facebook Shops / Instagram Shopping for in-app checkout
- Mercado Ads for retail media (growing $2B market in LATAM)

### WhatsApp Automation
- Manual WhatsApp float button → automate with WhatsApp Business API
- Use cases: abandoned cart recovery, order confirmations, shipping updates, promotions
- Several local providers (Woztell, Unify360, Wopy) can set this up

---

## P2 — Growth Features

### Customer Retention
- Order tracking page with branded UI and real-time status
- Push notifications for order updates
- Email automation flows: welcome, browse abandonment, post-purchase follow-up, re-engagement
- Loyalty program: points per purchase, birthday rewards, referral bonuses

### Personalization
- Customer segmentation (new vs returning, high vs low spend, category preference)
- Product recommendations based on browsing/purchase history
- Personalized email content

### B2B & Wholesale
- Bulk pricing tiers (e.g., 10+ units, 50+ units)
- B2B registration flow (request quote, net terms)
- Corporate account management

---

## P3 — Long-term

### Experience & Platform
- PWA with service worker for offline mode and installability
- Rental booking system (specific items, date range, deposit)
- Multi-warehouse / multi-location inventory
- Real-time POS-to-website inventory sync
- Advanced analytics dashboard with revenue forecasting
", "path": "/root/elviajero/docs/09-market-intelligence/03-ecommerce-features-gap-analysis.md"}

{"content": "# El Viajero — SEO, Marketing & Customer Acquisition Research

Date: May 8, 2026

---

## 1. Digital Landscape (Paraguay)

- 5.62M internet users (81.3% penetration)
- 81% of e-commerce sales from mobile
- 46% of shoppers use WhatsApp for purchases
- $700M e-commerce market, growing 32% YoY
- 77% of sales go to foreign stores → local stores have competitive advantage

### Social Media Users (Paraguay)
| Platform | Users | Penetration |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Facebook | 3.65-4.80M | 53-63% |
| Instagram | 2.60-2.72M | 37-38% |
| TikTok | 3.44M (18+) | 75% of adults |
| Messenger | 3.85M | 51% |
| LinkedIn | 1.09M | 14% |

---

## 2. Local SEO Priorities (Immediate)

### Google Business Profile
- #1 factor for local search visibility in Paraguay
- Claim/verify with accurate NAP
- Upload photos, maintain hours, solicit reviews
- 70% more likely to visit with complete GMB

### Local Directories
1. Paginas Amarillas Paraguay (paginasamarillas.com.py)
2. Infonegocios Paraguay (infonegocios.com.py)
3. Camara Nacional de Comercio y Servicios (cncsp.org.py)
4. Yellow Pages Paraguay

### Language-Specific SEO
- Paraguayan Spanish is distinct from Mexican/Argentinian Spanish
- Bilingual strategy: Spanish (formal/business) + Guarani (cultural/personal)
- 68% of Paraguayan brands prioritize localized content
- Localized content increases engagement by 35%
- Guarani phrases build authenticity and trust

---

## 3. MercadoLibre Strategy

- 65% market share in Paraguay e-commerce
- 11-17% commission depending on category
- Mercado Pago handles payments (70% of LATAM digital payments)
- 60% of purchases use installment plans
- Mercado Ads ACOS targets: 10-15% (profitability), 20-30% (growth)
- Seasonal events: Hot Sale (May), Black Friday (Nov), Christmas (Dec)

---

## 4. WhatsApp Marketing

### Why it's critical
- 46% of shoppers use WhatsApp as a shopping channel
- Highest open/read rates of any channel
- Average Paraguayan shopper uses 2.5 channels

### Automation tools available in Paraguay
| Tool | Best for | Price |
|------|----------|-------|
| Wopy | E-commerce + WhatsApp ordering + QR + delivery | Local |
| Unify360 | CRM + WhatsApp API + chatbots | Mid |
| Woztell | ChatGPT + WhatsApp | SMB |
| ChatPro/Kommo | Omnichannel CRM | Mid |

### Use cases to implement
1. Abandoned cart recovery (up to 35% recovery rate)
2. Order confirmation and tracking
3. Payment confirmation
4. Promotional campaigns
5. 24/7 customer support chatbot

---

## 5. Paid Ads in LATAM

### Meta Ads (Facebook/Instagram)
- Dynamic Ads, retargeting, lookalike audiences
- Must set up Meta Conversion API + GA4 (Pixel alone is insufficient)
- UGC content builds trust
- Installment options (cuotas) should be promoted in ads

### Google Ads
- 2x-8x ROI average reported
- Combine with SEO for immediate + sustainable results
- Google dominates search in Paraguay

### Mercado Ads (Retail Media)
- Fastest-growing digital ad format in LATAM
- $2B in 2024, projected $5.45B by 2028
- Some categories have highest ROAS globally

---

## 6. Content Marketing

### Pillars for El Viajero
1. Destination guides ("Los 10 Mejores Lugares para Acampar en Paraguay")
2. Gear reviews and comparisons
3. How-to content ("Como Armar tu Carpa en 5 Minutos")
4. Safety and skills content
5. Community stories and UGC
6. Seasonal content (summer prep, rainy season gear)

### Video content
- 78% of consumers prefer video
- Short-form (Reels, TikTok) is dominant format
- Tutorials and product reviews perform best
- YouTube for long-form gear reviews and destination vlogs

### Influencer marketing
- 3,000+ active influencer profiles in Paraguay
- 67% of consumers trust local influencer recommendations
- Micro-influencers (5K-50K) outperform macro for conversion
- Top niches relevant: travel & lifestyle (12%, 20% growth), eco-conscious (3%, 40% growth)

---

## 7. Voice Search (Untapped Opportunity)

- 60%+ of searches projected to be voice by 2025
- 50% of voice searches have local intent
- Target conversational long-tail queries ("Donde comprar carpas en Asuncion?")
- FAQ sections, featured snippets, schema markup all help

---

## 8. Mobile-First UX Requirements

- 81% of e-commerce sales from mobile
- <3s load time (53% leave if >3s, each second costs 20% conversions)
- Thumb-friendly navigation, sticky CTAs
- 80% of LATAM users don't feel confident sharing personal info online → trust signals critical
- WebP/AVIF images, lazy loading, CDN

---

## 9. Email/SMS Regulatory Landscape

| Law | Status | Key Requirements |
|-----|--------|-----------------|
| Law 6534/2020 | Active | Consent required, 2-year records, opt-out |
| Law 7593/25 | Nov 2027 | GDPR-aligned, DPO, breach notification, DPIA |

- SMS opt-out keywords required: STOP, PARAR, BASTA, ANICHEMA (Guarani)
- Consent must be available in Spanish AND Guarani
- Start compliance preparation NOW for Law 7593/25

---

## 10. Gap Analysis — Priority Actions

### Immediate (Week 1-2)
1. Google Search Console setup
2. Google Analytics 4 with enhanced e-commerce
3. Google Business Profile creation/optimization
4. Register on Paraguayan directories
5. Facebook Shops / Instagram Shopping setup

### Short-term (Month 1-2)
6. Product, BreadcrumbList, FAQ, Review schema
7. Spanish SEO content (local keywords)
8. Meta Ads campaign with WhatsApp integration
9. MercadoLibre seller account

### Medium-term (Month 2-3)
10. WhatsApp Business API automation
11. Abandoned cart via WhatsApp
12. Micro-influencer partnerships
13. TikTok content strategy
14. Guarani content testing

### Long-term (Month 3-6)
15. Mobile-first UX optimization
16. Core Web Vitals
17. Law 7593/25 compliance
18. Link-building campaign
19. Email automation + segmentation
20. SMS marketing (with compliance)
", "path": "/root/elviajero/docs/09-market-intelligence/04-seo-marketing-research.md"}

{"content": "# El Viajero — Business & Growth Opportunities Research

Date: May 8, 2026

---

## 1. Market Context

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Paraguay e-commerce market | $700M (2024), projected $1.5B (2027) |
| YoY growth | 32% |
| Domestic tourism growth (2025) | +103% |
| International tourists (2025) | 2.25M (+84% YoY) |
| LATAM adventure travel market | $39B |
| Camping goods LATAM growth | 2.2% CAGR volume, 2.5% CAGR value |

---

## 2. Growth Opportunity Matrix

| # | Opportunity | Effort | Impact | Timeline | Priority |
|---|-------------|--------|--------|----------|----------|
| 1 | Local pickup experience | Low | Medium | 1-2 weeks | HIGH |
| 2 | WhatsApp commerce automation | Low | High | 1-2 weeks | HIGH |
| 3 | B2B corporate sales | Low | Medium | 2-4 weeks | MEDIUM |
| 4 | Bulk/custom orders (embroidery) | Low | Low | 2-4 weeks | LOW |
| 5 | Warranty & repair services | Medium | Medium | 1-2 months | HIGH |
| 6 | Rental program | Medium | Medium | 1-2 months | HIGH |
| 7 | Content marketing | Medium | High | Ongoing | HIGH |
| 8 | Social media strategy | Medium | High | Ongoing | MEDIUM |
| 9 | Affiliate program | Low | Low-Med | 1 month | MEDIUM |
| 10 | Wholesale to other stores | Medium | Medium | 3-6 months | LOW |
| 11 | Cross-border (Brazil) | High | Very High | 3-6 months | MEDIUM |
| 12 | Cross-border (Argentina) | High | High | 3-6 months | LOW |
| 13 | Guided trips/experiences | High | Medium | 3-6 months | LOW |

---

## 3. Cross-Border to Brazil (Highest Potential)

### Why Paraguay wins
- Lowest tax burden in South America (15% of GDP)
- Brazilians can cross with $500 tax-free (land) or $1,000 (air)
- Brazilian outdoor gear is 30-50% more expensive locally
- PIX has 140M+ active users in Brazil

### Strategy
1. Open pickup point in Ciudad del Este (near Friendship Bridge)
2. Accept BRL + PIX payments
3. Create Portuguese-language content
4. Partner with 3XS International (UPS, 24h customs clearance)
5. Use ParaguayBox/NextParcel for last-mile

### Cross-border to Argentina
- Argentina eliminated taxes on overseas purchases up to $400
- Import limit raised to $3,000
- Argentine outdoor gear 30-50% more expensive
- List on Mercado Libre Argentina (24.8M monthly visits, $9.92B GMV)
- Use nocnoc platform for simplified cross-border

**Revenue potential:** Cross-border could add 30-50% incremental sales within 12-18 months.

---

## 4. B2B Corporate Sales

### Market
- MICE segment: $500M economic impact in 2025, 236 events
- Companies running retreats, team-building, field activities

### Product
- "Company Retreat in a Box" — bulk camping kits for 10-50 people
- Volume discounts starting at 10+ units
- Target: HR departments, event planners, schools, scouts, churches, sports clubs

### Revenue projection
- Single corporate order of 20 full kits at ~$100/kit = $2,000
- Target 5-10 corporate clients/month

---

## 5. Warranty & Repair Services (Blue Ocean)

**No dedicated outdoor gear repair exists in MRA/Asuncion area.**

### Services
- Warranty facilitation (ship to manufacturers, $5-10 handling fee)
- In-house repairs: zipper replacement, tent patches, strap repairs, sleeping bag restoration
- Gear restoration: DWR reapplication, down bag cleaning
- Custom modifications

### Financials
- Target: 30-50 repairs/month
- Average ticket: $15-20
- Monthly revenue: $450-$1,000
- Messaging: "Keep your gear alive" (sustainability angle)

---

## 6. Rental Program

### Pricing model (Chilean Patagonia benchmark)
| Item | Daily | Weekly |
|------|-------|--------|
| Family tent (6-8p) | $15 | $60 |
| 2-person tent | $8 | $30 |
| Sleeping bag | $5 | $20 |
| Full family kit | $40 | $150 |

### ROI
- A $200 tent rented 20x/year = $400 revenue (200% year 1)
- 10 rental transactions/week at $25 avg = $13,000/year
- Requires 10-15 core rental items to start

---

## 7. Competitor Snapshot

| Competitor | Founded | Location | Key Advantage |
|-----------|---------|----------|---------------|
| Camping 44 | 1990 | Asuncion | Largest, 34-year reputation |
| Chaco Outdoors | 2008 | Loma Plata + MRA | Direct neighbor, hunting/fishing |
| El Mohicano | — | Online | Shopify, camping+fishing |
| Decathlon PY | — | Asuncion | Cheapest prices, biggest catalog |
| CellShop | — | CDE | Premium brands, 12 installments |

**El Viajero's advantages:** Modern (2018), multi-category, physical+e-commerce hybrid, strategic MRA location.

---

## 8. 90-Day Action Plan

### Month 1 — Quick Wins
- WhatsApp Business API for automated order confirmations
- Designated pickup area with signage
- Instagram content calendar (3-5 posts/week)
- Corporate sales one-pager PDF
- 2 blog posts targeting local keywords

### Month 2 — Core Services
- Rental program with 10 initial items
- Repair service (partner with seamstress or train staff)
- Affiliate program with 5 micro-influencers
- YouTube channel with 3 videos
- Real-time inventory sync (in-store ↔ website)

### Month 3 — Growth & Cross-Border
- Evaluate CDE pickup point or courier partnership
- Portuguese-language content for Brazil
- Contact 3XS International for logistics
- Accept BRL + PIX payments
- Outreach to 10 corporate clients
- Launch "Comunidad Outdoor Paraguay" Facebook Group

{"content": "# El Viajero — Executive Summary: All Areas of Improvement

Date: May 8, 2026

This document consolidates all research findings into a single prioritized roadmap.

---

## 5 Domains × 40+ Improvement Areas

### A. TECHNOLOGY & ARCHITECTURE (10 areas)

| # | Area | Current State | Target State | Priority |
|---|------|--------------|--------------|----------|
| A1 | @ai-whisperers packages | 17 packages, 11 unused, 6 duplicated | Remove all, inline the 2 used ones | P1 |
| A2 | Private npm registry | .npmrc with GitHub PAT, breaks CI without VPN | Eliminate dependency | P1 |
| A3 | Content duplication | Products duplicated in es.json (71KB) and Supabase | Single source of truth (Supabase) | P2 |
| A4 | Dockerfile optimization | 365MB image, TypeScript shipped, ARG vs ENV confusion | Slim to 250MB, runtime env vars | P2 |
| A5 | Security: keys in version control | Supabase keys in docker-compose.yml (committed) | Docker secrets or .env only | P0 |
| A6 | Security: CSP header | Missing | Add to next.config.ts | P2 |
| A7 | Build pipeline | Manual deploy.sh, no CI/CD | Automated CI/CD | P2 |
| A8 | Test coverage | 2 Jest test files for 88 pages | Minimum smoke tests per page | P2 |
| A9 | Performance monitoring | None | GA4 + GSC + CWV monitoring | P1 |
| A10 | Error handling | Basic try/catch in API routes | Structured error responses, error boundaries | P2 |

### B. E-COMMERCE FEATURES (12 areas)

| # | Area | Current State | Target State | Priority |
|---|------|--------------|--------------|----------|
| B1 | **Live payments** | Pagopar/Bancard/Stripe keys not set | Activate Pagopar primary | **P0** |
| B2 | **Abandoned cart recovery** | API exists, not active | WhatsApp + email recovery flows | **P0** |
| B3 | **Shipping calculator** | Missing | Carrier integration with live rates | **P0** |
| B4 | Order tracking UX | Basic status page | Branded tracking with timeline + notifications | P1 |
| B5 | PWA / offline mode | Missing | Service worker, install prompt, offline cart | P2 |
| B6 | Push notifications | Missing | Browser push for order updates | P2 |

| B9 | Loyalty program | None | Points per purchase, referral, birthday rewards | P3 |

### D. BUSINESS GROWTH (8 areas)

| D1 | Cross-border (Brazil) | Nothing | CDE pickup, BRL+PIX, Portuguese content | P2 |
| D2 | B2B corporate sales | Nothing | Retreat kits, volume pricing, LinkedIn | P2 |
| D3 | Warranty & repair | Nothing | In-house repair + warranty handling | P2 |
| D4 | Gear rental program | Nothing | 10-15 items for rent, daily/weekly pricing | P2 |
| D5 | Content marketing | 6 blog posts | 50 articles, YouTube channel, destination guides | P2 |
| D6 | Affiliate program | Nothing | 10% commission for micro-influencers | P2 |
| D7 | Social media strategy | Inconsistent | 3-5x/week Instagram, daily Facebook, 2-3x/week TikTok | P2 |
| D8 | Cross-border (Argentina) | Nothing | Mercado Libre Argentina, nocnoc | P3 |

### E. COMPLIANCE & REGULATORY (3 areas)

**Top 3 things blocking revenue today:**

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.
Let me review the session and check which skills were loaded/consulted.

messages.content:
{"success": true, "name": "nextjs-docker-swarm", "description": "Build, deploy, and operate Next.js apps on Docker Swarm with Traefik ingress — covers image builds with private npm registries, Supabase key management, multi-replica deploys, and production debugging.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: nextjs-docker-swarm
description: Build, deploy, and operate Next.js apps on Docker Swarm with Traefik ingress — covers image builds with private npm registries, Supabase key management, multi-replica deploys, and production debugging.
---

# Next.js Docker Swarm Deployment

Class-level skill for deploying any Next.js app to Docker Swarm with Traefik. Covers patterns shared across client sites (El Viajero, Nexa Paraguay, etc.).

## Project Layout

Typical structure:
```
/root/<project>/
├── Dockerfile            # Multi-stage: deps -> builder -> runner
├── docker-compose.yml    # Swarm stack definition
├── deploy.sh             # build + docker build + stack deploy
├── .env.local            # NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars
├── .env                  # Secrets (non-public)
├── .npmrc                # GitHub Packages auth (@ai-whisperers:*)
├── content/es.json       # Static content (may contain build-time config)
└── CLAUDE.md             # Agent guide
```

## Dockerfile Pattern

Standard multi-stage for Next.js standalone output:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
ARG NODE_AUTH_TOKEN
ENV NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=$NODE_AUTH_TOKEN
RUN echo "@scope:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com" > .npmrc && \
    echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${NODE_AUTH_TOKEN}" >> .npmrc && \
    echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:always-auth=true" >> .npmrc && \
    npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
RUN apk add --no-cache wget curl
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:3000/api/health || exit 1
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
CMD node server.js
```

## Build & Deploy

```bash
# Build with all required build-args
docker build \
  -t <project>:prod \
  --build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="ghp_xxx" \
  --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL="https://xxx.supabase.co" \
  --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="eyJ..." \
  .

# Deploy stack
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack-name> --with-registry-auth --resolve-image always
```

## Compose Template (Traefik + Swarm)

```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: <project>:prod
    ports:
      - "3000"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://xxx.supabase.co
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
      - SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=eyJ...
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://example.com
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.rule=Host(`example.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.<name>.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '0.5'
          memory: 512M
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
```

## Orphan Container Detection

Before deploying, check for orphan `docker-compose` containers that can interfere with Swarm:

```bash
# List ALL containers matching the project name (not just swarm tasks)
docker ps -a --filter "name=<project>" --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}"

# Look for containers named like <project>-web-2 (compose-style) vs <project>_web.1 (swarm-style)
# Compose-style containers are NOT managed by Swarm and will NOT be updated by stack deploy
```

If you find orphans:

```bash
docker rm -f <orphan-container-name>
# Also remove old images they reference, so nothing can restart them
docker rmi <old-image-id>
```

Then verify only swarm replicas are running:

```bash
docker service ps <stack> --format "table {{.Name}}\t{{.CurrentState}}\t{{.Image}}"
```

## Critical Pitfalls

### 1. NODE_AUTH_TOKEN Not Passed to Docker Build

The Dockerfile expects `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` as a build arg for `npm install` from GitHub Packages. If missing, `npm install` gets a 401 from the package registry.

**Always pass it**: `--build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="$(grep _authToken .npmrc | sed 's/.*=//')"`

Also see `references/deployment-pitfalls.md` for additional pitfalls not covered above.

### 8. NEXT_PUBLIC_* Not Passed to Runner Stage

The Dockerfile passes `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` as build args to `builder` stage. The `runner` stage also needs `ENV` entries for these (copied from `builder` via `$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`). If the runner stage doesn't have them, the server can't connect to Supabase at runtime.

**Verification**: `docker inspect <container> | grep NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE`

### 9. Pages Router vs App Router — What Changed in Next.js 16

Next.js 16 defaults to Turbopack. Pages Router `[[...slug]].tsx` silently produces zero output in some App Router configs. Key rules:

- **Both `pages/` and `app/` can coexist** but Next.js 16 prefers `pages/` if both exist. Delete `pages/` when migrating to App Router.
- **Middleware file is deprecated** in Next.js 16. Use `proxy` instead. The old `middleware.ts` still works but warns.
- **`[locale]` directory naming**: use `\[locale\]` in mkdir commands to prevent bash brace expansion from eating the brackets.
- **Dynamic imports work fine** with `'use client'` components and `dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: true })`.
- **No `next/router` in App Router**: use `next/navigation` (`usePathname`, `useSearchParams`) instead.

### 10. Partial Pre-Rendering (PPR) in Next.js 16

Enable with `cacheComponents: true` in next.config.js. Key rules for PPR:

- **Every uncached data access MUST be inside Suspense**. Wrap all data fetching components in `<Suspense fallback={...}>`.
- **All route segment configs are forbidden** (`revalidate`, `dynamicParams`, `dynamic`, `force-static`). PPR handles everything.
- **`generateStaticParams` is REQUIRED** for every dynamic route segment. Returns empty array if no params known.
- **`generateMetadata` must NOT access uncached data** (filesystem, DB). Return minimal metadata for dynamic routes.
- **`new Date()` in a server component** counts as uncached data. Hardcode static values (e.g., `'2026'` for year).
- **Blog post JSON format trap**: `posts.json` may be `{ "posts": [...] }` not a flat array. Always use `const list = posts.posts || posts` before calling `.find()` or `.filter()`.
- **`process.cwd()` in PPR context**: Works during build (equals repo root). In Docker, the standalone server runs from `/app`. Ensure filesystem paths use `join(process.cwd(), 'content/...')`.
- **Client components with `'use client'`** are fine inside Suspense — they stream in after the static shell renders.
- **Client components with `'use client'`** are fine inside Suspense — they stream in after the static shell renders.
- Build output shows `◐` (Partial Prerender) for PPR pages vs `●` (SSG) and `ƒ` (Dynamic).

### 3. Supabase Anon Key Regeneration

Supabase can regenerate the anon/publishable key in the project dashboard. The old key becomes invalid silently — users see "Invalid API key" when trying to log in.

**Where the key lives (must update ALL):**
- `.env.local` — `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=`
- `docker-compose.yml` — environment section
- `content/*.json` — if the app reads config from content JSON at build time

**Fix**: Get the current key from Supabase dashboard (Settings > API) and update all 3 locations. Redeploy.

### 4. Content JSON as Config Store

Some apps read Supabase URL/anon key from `content/es.json` via static JSON imports:
```typescript
import content from '@/content/es.json'
const supabaseUrl = content.supabase.url
```
This means the key is **baked into the Next.js build** at `npm run build` time. Changing `.env.local` alone won't fix it — you must also rebuild.

### 5. Build Fails But Old Containers Keep Running

If `docker build` fails, the old image stays tagged as `:prod`. The existing containers continue running on the old image. This is safer than compose which would restart failed containers.

### 6. Traefik Labels at Wrong Level

Traefik labels must be at `service.deploy.labels` (in the `deploy:` section), NOT at `service.labels`. Docker Swarm ignores `service.labels` when running in swarm mode.

### 7. JSON Corruption with sed/patch

Large JSON files (200KB+) are fragile with sed/patch. Prefer `python3 -c "import json; json.load(...); json.dump(...)"` for edits.

## Database Troubleshooting

### Admin login shows "Invalid API key"

1. Check the anon key against what Supabase dashboard shows (Settings > API)
2. If key was regenerated: update `.env.local`, `docker-compose.yml`, and `content/*.json`
3. Rebuild Docker image: `docker build --build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=... --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=... .`
4. Redeploy: `docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack>`
5. Verify: `curl -s https://example.com/api/auth | head`

### Admin login shows "Invalid login credentials"

The Supabase key is correct but the password is wrong. Two options:
- Use Supabase Admin API to reset: `curl -X PUT "$SUPABASE_URL/auth/v1/admin/users/$USER_ID" -H "apikey: $SERVICE_KEY" -H "Authorization: Bearer $SERVICE_KEY" -d '{"password":"newpass"}'`
- Send a password reset email via the app's recovery flow

### Login works but admin panel shows no data

Check admin API routes use `createAdminClient()` from the auth package which reads `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` from environment. If that env var is missing in the container, profile queries fail due to RLS policies.

## Adding to CLAUDE.md

Every project should have a `CLAUDE.md` at the repo root with:
- Quick links (live URL, repo, local path, VPS, Traefik host rules)
- Architecture summary (CF -> VPS -> Traefik -> service)
- Pages list
- Design system notes
- Build & deploy commands
- Critical patterns (e.g., WhatsApp-first ordering)

{"success": true, "name": "nextjs-docker-swarm", "description": "Build, deploy, and operate Next.js apps on Docker Swarm with Traefik ingress — covers image builds with private npm registries, Supabase key management, multi-replica deploys, and production debugging.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: nextjs-docker-swarm
description: Build, deploy, and operate Next.js apps on Docker Swarm with Traefik ingress — covers image builds with private npm registries, Supabase key management, multi-replica deploys, and production debugging.
---

# Next.js Docker Swarm Deployment

Class-level skill for deploying any Next.js app to Docker Swarm with Traefik. Covers patterns shared across client sites (El Viajero, Nexa Paraguay, etc.).

## Project Layout

Typical structure:
```
/root/<project>/
├── Dockerfile            # Multi-stage: deps -> builder -> runner
├── docker-compose.yml    # Swarm stack definition
├── deploy.sh             # build + docker build + stack deploy
├── .env.local            # NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars
├── .env                  # Secrets (non-public)
├── .npmrc                # GitHub Packages auth (@ai-whisperers:*)
├── content/es.json       # Static content (may contain build-time config)
└── CLAUDE.md             # Agent guide
```

## Dockerfile Pattern

Standard multi-stage for Next.js standalone output:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
ARG NODE_AUTH_TOKEN
ENV NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=$NODE_AUTH_TOKEN
RUN echo "@scope:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com" > .npmrc && \
    echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${NODE_AUTH_TOKEN}" >> .npmrc && \
    echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:always-auth=true" >> .npmrc && \
    npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
RUN apk add --no-cache wget curl
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:3000/api/health || exit 1
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
CMD node server.js
```

## Build & Deploy

```bash
# Build with all required build-args
docker build \
  -t <project>:prod \
  --build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="ghp_xxx" \
  --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL="https://xxx.supabase.co" \
  --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="eyJ..." \
  .

# Deploy stack
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack-name> --with-registry-auth --resolve-image always
```

## Compose Template (Traefik + Swarm)

```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: <project>:prod
    ports:
      - "3000"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://xxx.supabase.co
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
      - SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=eyJ...
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://example.com
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.rule=Host(`example.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.<name>.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.<name>.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '0.5'
          memory: 512M
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
```

## Orphan Container Detection

Before deploying, check for orphan `docker-compose` containers that can interfere with Swarm:

```bash
# List ALL containers matching the project name (not just swarm tasks)
docker ps -a --filter "name=<project>" --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}"

# Look for containers named like <project>-web-2 (compose-style) vs <project>_web.1 (swarm-style)
# Compose-style containers are NOT managed by Swarm and will NOT be updated by stack deploy
```

If you find orphans:

```bash
# 1. Identify ALL containers — look for compose-style names (project-name-N) amid swarm-style (project_service.N)
docker ps -a --filter "name=<project>" --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.CreatedAt}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}"

# 2. Check if the orphan is on the same Docker network (it will have its own IP and may be routable)
docker network inspect agent-net --format '{{range .Containers}}{{.Name}} -> {{.IPv4Address}}{{println}}{{end}}' | grep <project>

# 3. Kill the orphan
docker rm -f <orphan-container-name>

# 4. Remove old images so nothing (healthcheck crons, docker-compose restart policies) can revive them
docker rmi <old-image-id>

# 5. Verify only swarm replicas remain
docker service ps <stack> --format "table {{.Name}}\t{{.CurrentState}}\t{{.Image}}"
docker ps --filter "name=<project>" --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.CreatedAt}}\t{{.Status}}"
```

## Critical Pitfalls

### 1. NODE_AUTH_TOKEN Not Passed to Docker Build

The Dockerfile expects `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` as a build arg for `npm install` from GitHub Packages. If missing, `npm install` gets a 401 from the package registry.

**Always pass it**: `--build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="$(grep _authToken .npmrc | sed 's/.*=//')"`

Also see `references/deployment-pitfalls.md` for additional pitfalls not covered above.

### 8. NEXT_PUBLIC_* Not Passed to Runner Stage

The Dockerfile passes `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` as build args to `builder` stage. The `runner` stage also needs `ENV` entries for these (copied from `builder` via `$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`). If the runner stage doesn't have them, the server can't connect to Supabase at runtime.

**Verification**: `docker inspect <container> | grep NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE`

### 9. Pages Router vs App Router — What Changed in Next.js 16

Next.js 16 defaults to Turbopack. Pages Router `[[...slug]].tsx` silently produces zero output in some App Router configs. Key rules:

- **Both `pages/` and `app/` can coexist** but Next.js 16 prefers `pages/` if both exist. Delete `pages/` when migrating to App Router.
- **Middleware file is deprecated** in Next.js 16. Use `proxy` instead. The old `middleware.ts` still works but warns.
- **`[locale]` directory naming**: use `\[locale\]` in mkdir commands to prevent bash brace expansion from eating the brackets.
- **Dynamic imports work fine** with `'use client'` components and `dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: true })`.
- **No `next/router` in App Router**: use `next/navigation` (`usePathname`, `useSearchParams`) instead.

### 10. Partial Pre-Rendering (PPR) in Next.js 16

Enable with `cacheComponents: true` in next.config.js. Key rules for PPR:

- **Every uncached data access MUST be inside Suspense**. Wrap all data fetching components in `<Suspense fallback={...}>`.
- **All route segment configs are forbidden** (`revalidate`, `dynamicParams`, `dynamic`, `force-static`). PPR handles everything.
- **`generateStaticParams` is REQUIRED** for every dynamic route segment. Returns empty array if no params known.
- **`generateMetadata` must NOT access uncached data** (filesystem, DB). Return minimal metadata for dynamic routes.
- **`new Date()` in a server component** counts as uncached data. Hardcode static values (e.g., `'2026'` for year).
- **Blog post JSON format trap**: `posts.json` may be `{ "posts": [...] }` not a flat array. Always use `const list = posts.posts || posts` before calling `.find()` or `.filter()`.
- **`process.cwd()` in PPR context**: Works during build (equals repo root). In Docker, the standalone server runs from `/app`. Ensure filesystem paths use `join(process.cwd(), 'content/...')`.
- **Client components with `'use client'`** are fine inside Suspense — they stream in after the static shell renders.
- **Client components with `'use client'`** are fine inside Suspense — they stream in after the static shell renders.
- Build output shows `◐` (Partial Prerender) for PPR pages vs `●` (SSG) and `ƒ` (Dynamic).

### 3. Supabase Anon Key Regeneration

Supabase can regenerate the anon/publishable key in the project dashboard. The old key becomes invalid silently — users see "Invalid API key" when trying to log in.

**Fastest fix**: Use Supabase MCP to get the current keys directly:
```bash
# Via the supabase MCP tool (if available in this session)
mcp_supabase_get_publishable_keys
# Returns both the legacy anon key and the new publishable key
```

**Where the key lives (must update ALL):**
- `.env.local` — `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=`
- `docker-compose.yml` — environment section
- `content/*.json` — if the app reads config from content JSON at build time

**Verification**: Test the key directly against Supabase:
```bash
curl -s -X POST "https://<project>.supabase.co/auth/v1/token?grant_type=password" \
  -H "apikey: <new-anon-key>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"[REDACTED_EMAIL]","password":"wrongpass"}'
# "Invalid API key" = key still wrong
# "Invalid login credentials" = key works, password wrong
```

**Fix**: Get the current key from Supabase dashboard (Settings > API) and update all 3 locations. Redeploy.

### 4. Content JSON as Config Store

Some apps read Supabase URL/anon key from `content/es.json` via static JSON imports:
```typescript
import content from '@/content/es.json'
const supabaseUrl = content.supabase.url
```
This means the key is **baked into the Next.js build** at `npm run build` time. Changing `.env.local` alone won't fix it — you must also rebuild.

### 5. Build Fails But Old Containers Keep Running

If `docker build` fails, the old image stays tagged as `:prod`. The existing containers continue running on the old image. This is safer than compose which would restart failed containers.

### 6. Traefik Labels at Wrong Level

Traefik labels must be at `service.deploy.labels` (in the `deploy:` section), NOT at `service.labels`. Docker Swarm ignores `service.labels` when running in swarm mode.

### 7. JSON Corruption with sed/patch

Large JSON files (200KB+) are fragile with sed/patch. Prefer `python3 -c "import json; json.load(...); json.dump(...)"` for edits.

## Database Troubleshooting

### Admin login shows "Invalid API key"

1. Check the anon key against what Supabase dashboard shows (Settings > API)
2. If key was regenerated: update `.env.local`, `docker-compose.yml`, and `content/*.json`
3. Rebuild Docker image: `docker build --build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=... --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=... .`
4. Redeploy: `docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <stack>`
5. Verify: `curl -s https://example.com/api/auth | head`

### Admin login shows "Invalid login credentials"

The Supabase key is correct but the password is wrong. Two options:
- Use Supabase Admin API to reset: `curl -X PUT "$SUPABASE_URL/auth/v1/admin/users/$USER_ID" -H "apikey: $SERVICE_KEY" -H "Authorization: Bearer $SERVICE_KEY" -d '{"password":"newpass"}'`
- Send a password reset email via the app's recovery flow

### Login works but admin panel shows no data

Check admin API routes use `createAdminClient()` from the auth package which reads `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` from environment. If that env var is missing in the container, profile queries fail due to RLS policies.

## Adding to CLAUDE.md

Every project should have a `CLAUDE.md` at the repo root with:
- Quick links (live URL, repo, local path, VPS, Traefik host rules)
- Architecture summary (CF -> VPS -> Traefik -> service)
- Pages list
- Design system notes
- Build & deploy commands
- Critical patterns (e.g., WhatsApp-first ordering)
